name: Orange-Walnut Madelines
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease and flour twenty-four 3-inch madeline molds. Set molds aside.
- Look at a kitchen specialty store for a madeline pan with 8 or 12 shapes.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, beat sugar and egg yolks with an electric mixer on medium to high speed about 30 seconds until thoroughly mixed.
4. Add melted butter, orange peel, orange juice, and vanilla. Beat on low speed until combined.
5. Sift together flour, baking powder, cardamom, baking soda, and salt.
6. Gradually add one-fourth of the flour mixture to the egg-yolk mixture, gently folding in each portion.
7. Continue adding the remaining flour mixture, again folding it in a few at a time.
8. Fold in walnuts and gently stir in egg whites until just combined.
9. Spoon batter into prepared molds, filling each mold about half full.
10. Bake in the preheated oven for 10 to 12 minutes or until edges are golden brown and tops spring back when lightly touched.
11. Cool cakes in molds for 1 minute. Using the point of a knife, loosen cakes from the molds; invert onto a wire rack.
12. Remove molds completely and allow cakes to cool on the rack.
13. Tightly cover and store at room temperature up to 3 days. Just before serving, sift powdered sugar over tops.
14. Makes 24.
